items.headline: Albemarle Stock And 2 More Shares Exposed To China Supply Chain Risks

items.headline: Bull of the Day: Albemarle (ALB)
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) that is the world's premier lithium producer. After a brutal two-year bear market in lithium, the cycle has turned positive. The stock has dropped aggressively over the last month, giving investors a potential entry point for a rall

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB) is the world's largest lithium producer. First-quarter 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% year over year to $664 million as lithium prices rebounded from 2025 lows, and adjusted earnings came in well ahead of what Wall Street had modeled. Albemarle

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Chinese spot prices have climbed back into the $23 per kilogram range, up from their $10 kg low in the fall of 2024, thanks to a restocking cycle by battery manufacturers. Analysts project a 4% global lithium supply deficit for 2026, which should act as a powerful tailwind for re

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is likely to continue growing volumes over the coming years from mostly brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at its Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs, RBC Capital Markets said. RBC now expects 2026 and 2027 EBITDA of $2.8 billion and $2.9
